title: Pregnancy-safe evening.
body: Cleanse, azelaic 10%, ceramide cream. The list of what you can do, not what you can't.

steps.title: Gentle cream cleanser
steps.body: Pregnancy is not the time to introduce a double cleanse. A single cream cleanser, lukewarm water, 30 seconds. If you wear mineral SPF + makeup, an oil cleanser before is fine — but check the ingredient list for retinyl palmitate, which crops up in some 'anti-aging' oils.

steps.title: Azelaic acid 10%
steps.body: The single active most worth keeping in pregnancy. Azelaic addresses both melasma and pregnancy-related acne flares without the safety questions of a retinoid or BHA. Apply to dry skin, wait 5 minutes before moisturiser. Stinging is normal in the first two weeks; usually resolves.

steps.title: Ceramide cream
steps.body: Pregnancy dryness — particularly in the second trimester — responds to a slightly richer ceramide cream than your usual moisturiser. The barrier is your front line; protect it with a thicker layer at night.
